capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “East stairwell lighting repair” in Building A and “Training room setup” in Building B, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete request records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. In the final answer, list the two records alphabetically by returned name before the comparison.
prompt:
The facilities operations lead needs a read-only request comparison. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create operations-check.md from complete request records, not search summaries or backing files. Use the genuine local ./requestctl executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect its built-in help before accessing request data. Do not inspect requests.sql, the runtime database, .protected/verify.py, setup_environment.py, or the execution journal to obtain request data, and do not write a wrapper to perform the task.

Find the request named “East stairwell lighting repair” in “Building A” and the request named “Training room setup” in “Building B”. Your first request-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-building searches concurrently as two separate direct ./requestctl processes, with no other request operation in that action. Wait for both results. Continue only after each search has returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. Your immediately following request-data assistant action must start both complete-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct ./requestctl processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. Do not guess an ID or retrieve a branch whose search was missing or ambiguous.

This is a read-only check. Do not invoke list, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter request records or notification state. Take every reported record field, including status and date, only from successful complete-record retrievals.

operations-check.md must contain exactly five nonblank lines with no heading or other prose. Sort the two record lines alphabetically by the names returned from the complete records, then use these shapes:
<name> | <stable ID> | <building> | <date> | <status> | <requester> | <details>
<name> | <stable ID> | <building> | <date> | <status> | <requester> | <details>
Status comparison: <same|different> (<alphabetically first status> vs <alphabetically second status>).
Date comparison: <earlier-name> is <N> day or days earlier (<earlier date> vs <later date>).
No changes made.

Use the singular word day only when N is 1. Run python3 -B .protected/verify.py after writing the file, leave protected files and service state unchanged, and return exactly the five file lines as your final answer.
